jokes.text: I was in the middle of bidding on a bag of bras on eBay
jokes.setup: Jack urgently calls Liz, suggesting something important
jokes.punchline: She reveals she was doing something absurdly mundane and specific
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character moment - specific, weird, and relatable in its mundanity

jokes.text: I saw that in a movie once, but in the movie, the guy was dead
jokes.setup: Jack mentions meeting with the boss could be good
jokes.punchline: Liz compares it to a movie where the similar scenario meant death
jokes.explanation: Smart misdirection and dark humor delivered with Liz's neurotic precision

jokes.text: I was sitting in that chair a minute ago. Nope. That was me. What can I say. I smell like leather.
jokes.setup: Confusion about who was sitting where
jokes.punchline: Hank's bizarre non-sequitur explanation about smelling like leather
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd character introduction - the logic is completely broken in a funny way

jokes.text: Well, I'll be Bake McBrided
jokes.setup: Hank expressing surprise or recognition
jokes.punchline: Made-up exclamation that sounds like a real phrase but isn't
jokes.explanation: Silly made-up phrase that establishes Hank's folksy character

jokes.text: Without Tracy, your show is like my cholesterol. The numbers are killing me.
jokes.setup: Comparing the show to something personal
jokes.punchline: Unexpected comparison to cholesterol with double meaning
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor that works on multiple levels - both ratings and health concerns

jokes.text: We got on Wikipedia this week
jokes.setup: Liz trying to argue the show is getting good media buzz
jokes.punchline: Wikipedia is presented as significant media coverage
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing how low the bar is for their 'success'

jokes.text: So you did 99 shows against all odds and reason
jokes.setup: Acknowledging they made it to 99 episodes
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment suggesting it shouldn't have happened
jokes.explanation: Perfect executive speak - sounds supportive while being completely dismissive

jokes.text: number 1 in its time slot among men 18 to 49 months left in prison
jokes.setup: Citing impressive demographic statistics
jokes.punchline: The demographic is absurdly specific and depressing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - starts like normal TV demo data then takes absurd turn

jokes.text: I thought this company was a family, but I guess it's that Austrian family and I am the girl in the basement and you are the dad who has been brutally...
jokes.setup: Liz comparing the company to a family
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to Josef Fritzl case with increasingly dark implications
jokes.explanation: Dark and topical reference that escalates uncomfortably before being cut off

jokes.text: Just like the Army doctor said to me after my weekend in Okinawa!
jokes.setup: Agreeing to give them one more shot
jokes.punchline: Comparison to military medical situation with dark implications
jokes.explanation: Good character beat establishing Hank's inappropriate oversharing

jokes.text: Jack Welch once smacked a pretzel out of my hand at the Super Bowl
jokes.setup: Explaining why KableTown is better than GE
jokes.punchline: Specific, petty example of corporate meanness
jokes.explanation: Absurdly specific and petty grievance that feels authentic to the character

jokes.text: Everybody dance now!
jokes.setup: Liz declaring she'll do whatever it takes 'Lemon style'
jokes.punchline: Her 'style' is just shouting a 90s dance song lyric
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment - trying to be energetic but falling back on pop culture

jokes.text: But you're going to get me another sandwich, or I'm going to cut your face up so bad, you'll have a chin. I will waste you! You'll have to go through this old bastard first! And I don't really think it's fair for me to be on a jury because I'm a hologram.
jokes.setup: Tracy being crazy and threatening
jokes.punchline: Escalates from violence to completely random hologram non-sequitur
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy insanity - threatening violence then hologram jury duty in same breath

jokes.text: He feels like a Bartram's Scrub-Hairstreak trying to crawl back into its silky cocoon which is a butterfly metaphor, by the way, and not, as I first thought, a list of African-American hair products
jokes.setup: Liz explaining Tracy's emotional state with butterfly metaphor
jokes.punchline: Reveals she initially thought the scientific name was hair products
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that works on multiple levels - scientific vs. commercial naming

jokes.text: Really? Is it from that pie place?
jokes.setup: Jack mentions having a very full plate
jokes.punchline: Liz takes the metaphor literally
jokes.explanation: Simple literal interpretation joke that fits Liz's food obsession

jokes.text: There is no pie
jokes.setup: Liz's confusion about the literal plate
jokes.punchline: Jack's deadpan clarification
jokes.explanation: Simple payoff to the pie misunderstanding

jokes.text: In 100 shows, we've done 'Pam' 107 times
jokes.setup: Celebrating 100 shows
jokes.punchline: They've somehow done one sketch more times than episodes exist
jokes.explanation: Good mathematical impossibility joke that shows the show's repetitive nature

jokes.text: That wasn't me. There used to be another guy. I don't think so.
jokes.setup: Danny reminiscing about 5 years of working together
jokes.punchline: Danny reveals he wasn't there, Jenna dismisses this reality
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about cast changes that Jenna refuses to acknowledge

jokes.text: I will choke you to death with your boyfriend's wig!
jokes.setup: Jenna being congratulated on the milestone
jokes.punchline: Immediate violent threat with oddly specific weapon choice
jokes.explanation: Typical Jenna over-reaction with specific insult about wig

jokes.text: If this turkey goes 100 episodes, I'll have your baby
jokes.setup: Flashback to Jenna's first day confidence
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific bet that's now come due
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the pregnancy plotline with ironic reversal

jokes.text: Wait, I saw that. How? Am I dead?
jokes.setup: Jenna seeing a flashback of herself
jokes.punchline: Questioning the logic of seeing her own past
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about TV narrative conventions from character's perspective

jokes.text: I better lay you across my grandmother's lap in the mating shed
jokes.setup: Danny acknowledging the 100 episode bet
jokes.punchline: Bizarre sexual reference involving grandmother and shed
jokes.explanation: Absurdly specific sexual imagery that's uncomfortable and weird

jokes.text: Albinos get to be watchers in the mating shed
jokes.setup: Jenna calling him an albino goon
jokes.punchline: Reference back to the mating shed with specific albino role
jokes.explanation: Callback that extends the weird mating shed mythology

jokes.text: Who wore it best? I did. I wore it best.
jokes.setup: Imagining pregnancy magazine comparison
jokes.punchline: Immediately answering her own rhetorical question with confidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna narcissism - can't even pose hypothetical without winning

jokes.text: Frank, but that bigger hat back on
jokes.setup: Liz giving pep talk to dispirited writers
jokes.punchline: Frank apparently has multiple sized hats for different moods
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag about Frank's depression hat system

jokes.text: Tonight, 'TGS' will not be the worst thing on television. It'll be John Stossel!
jokes.setup: Liz building up to inspirational statement about their show
jokes.punchline: They'll only be second worst, beaten by specific journalist
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection in pep talk - lowered expectations with specific target

jokes.text: Why am I showing you guys this? It's going to jinx everything. Plus, you're a couple of pervs.
jokes.setup: Guy showing photo of his girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Self-aware of both superstition and inappropriate audience
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing self-awareness about clichéd behavior

jokes.text: Isn't that show already half-dead?
jokes.setup: Learning they need to evacuate TGS
jokes.punchline: Suggesting evacuation won't change much
jokes.explanation: Simple joke about the show's poor reputation

jokes.text: See? I got a new hair. It's white and it hurts, but...
jokes.setup: Jack proving he has other things going on
jokes.punchline: Single hair as evidence of busy life, with concerning description
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - treating single hair as major life development

jokes.text: Other possible side-effects include hallucinations, revelation of secrets, telling of truths, flashbacks, headaches, nostalgia
jokes.setup: Listing dangerous effects of gas leak
jokes.punchline: List includes TV episode plot devices
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about the episode's own structure disguised as safety warning

jokes.text: Can you believe this is happening to me on my last day of work? My last day. I don't know if there was a party planned...
jokes.setup: Guy lamenting bad timing of gas leak
jokes.punchline: Fishing for information about retirement party
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment of subtle manipulation for retirement party info

jokes.text: I refuse to have long, gas-induced conversations with any of you people
jokes.setup: Jack deciding to evacuate
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging the episode's premise while rejecting it
jokes.explanation: Meta moment where character rejects the episode's own conceit

jokes.text: More like 100 little strokes
jokes.setup: Celebrating 100 shows
jokes.punchline: Pete's dark take comparing each show to minor brain damage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pete line - darkly funny and captures his beleaguered state

jokes.text: he didn't care if I watched TV during sex
jokes.setup: Liz nostalgically remembering ex-boyfriend Denis
jokes.punchline: Revealing inappropriate detail about their relationship
jokes.explanation: Good Liz overshare that reveals her priorities and dysfunction

jokes.text: Just follow these... troll penises!
jokes.setup: Liz confidently leading evacuation
jokes.punchline: Gas effects kick in causing hallucinations
jokes.explanation: Good gas leak effect moment with absurd hallucination

jokes.text: Why don't you shut your mouth, back that ass up, and make me a sandwich!
jokes.setup: Tracy on morning TV trying to be controversial
jokes.punchline: Extremely inappropriate demand on live television
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment but the offensive content is fairly predictable for Tracy's character.

jokes.text: I was hiding in a warehouse in Queens watching vintage pornography
jokes.setup: Tracy confessing he lied about going to Africa
jokes.punchline: Reveals pathetic and specific alternative activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy confession - specific, inappropriate, and completely undermines his image

jokes.text: It's new hair cuts, but you can make salads with them
jokes.setup: Explaining show segment 'no-cook cooking hair make-unders'
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical explanation that makes no logical sense
jokes.explanation: Absurd morning show segment that captures vapid TV perfectly

jokes.text: Broken eyboard alk show
jokes.setup: Frank presenting sketch ideas
jokes.punchline: Title shows missing keys on his keyboard
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag about Frank's broken equipment/lazy writing

jokes.text: Is it a drink? Is it a drug? Is it a bandage you put on a barn?
jokes.setup: Tracy confused about Farm Aid
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd guesses about what it could be
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy confusion with escalating absurd interpretations

jokes.text: See that's the kind of lazy standup I'll never do again!
jokes.setup: Tracy making absurd guesses about Farm Aid
jokes.punchline: Immediately criticizing his own joke style
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment where character critiques his own comedic performance in real time

jokes.text: 'F' you, L.L. Spells 'full'! Because you're full of B.S., Liz Lemon!
jokes.setup: Tracy getting angry at Liz
jokes.punchline: Convoluted spelling-based insult
jokes.explanation: Cleverly convoluted Tracy logic. The spelling explanation is unnecessarily complex and perfect for his character.

jokes.text: No! And I wasn't even drinking anything
jokes.setup: Jenna asking if Liz thinks she'd be a good mother
jokes.punchline: Emphatic no with clarification about lack of spit-take
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat with meta-reference to spit-take comedy

jokes.text: I'm in my 40s, very difficult, and not that good at playing 'la realitee'
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining why she might stop working
jokes.punchline: Honest self-assessment with pretentious pronunciation of 'reality'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment - rare honesty coupled with pretension

jokes.text: Who will grow up to be a little gay fancy man
jokes.setup: Liz warning Jenna about having a child
jokes.punchline: Jenna's positive reaction to having a gay son
jokes.explanation: Typical Jenna response - sees having gay son as accessory/validation

jokes.text: I'm getting to old for this 'shh' sound that comes from this gas pipe
jokes.setup: Worker dealing with gas leak
jokes.punchline: References action movie tropes about being too old
jokes.explanation: Good meta reference applied to mundane situation

jokes.text: Who are you? You're so handsome.
jokes.setup: Jack hallucinating from gas leak
jokes.punchline: Complimenting his own reflection/hallucination
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack vanity moment. Even when hallucinating, he's attracted to himself.

jokes.text: It's called 'power clashing,' and I do it because I can
jokes.setup: Regular Jack criticizing alternate Jack's fashion choices
jokes.punchline: Alternate Jack's arrogant justification for bad fashion
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast showing alternate Jack's different values

jokes.text: Town, Donaghy, and that's not funny. It was funny when I said it on my syndicated radio show.
jokes.setup: Regular Jack making joke about 'Kable Clown'
jokes.punchline: Alternate Jack's superior dismissal and credential flex
jokes.explanation: Good alternate universe competitiveness with media platform bragging

jokes.text: Sharks don't have claws. You don't even know what a shark is anymore!
jokes.setup: Regular Jack claiming to still have claws like a shark
jokes.punchline: Alternate Jack's literal correction revealing metaphor breakdown
jokes.explanation: Good moment showing how far regular Jack has fallen from business acumen

jokes.text: She became your focus, at the expense of your own ambition. ... Lemon was a black hole unworthy of our mentorship.
jokes.setup: Explaining what went wrong with real Jack
jokes.punchline: Brutal assessment of Liz as a 'black hole' who ruined Jack
jokes.explanation: Sharp character analysis that reframes the entire show dynamic. 'Black hole' is a perfect metaphor.

jokes.text: What the hell is a Pwomp?
jokes.setup: Alternate Jack seeing TGS merchandise
jokes.punchline: Confusion about show's recurring joke
jokes.explanation: Callback to recurring sketch showing outsider perspective

jokes.text: What the hell is a Pwomp? ... It's when two fat people... I don't care!
jokes.setup: Alternate Jack questioning TGS merchandise
jokes.punchline: Jack starts to explain the running gag but gets cut off
jokes.explanation: Great callback usage and character moment. The dismissal before explanation is perfect alternate Jack.

jokes.text: I visited my rescue chimp for his birthday last week, and another chimp got mad and bit my nipple off. He played with it, and then he kissed it, and then he ate it.
jokes.setup: Lutz explaining why he can't take his shirt off
jokes.punchline: Increasingly disturbing details about chimp encounter
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lutz overshare - starts sympathetic then becomes horrifying and absurd

jokes.text: I visited my rescue chimp for his birthday last week, and another chimp got mad and bit my nipple off. He played with it, and then he kissed it, and then he ate it.
jokes.setup: Lutz explaining why he won't take his shirt off
jokes.punchline: Detailed description of bizarre chimp encounter
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lutz moment - bizarre, specific, and escalating. The detailed sequence of chimp behavior is brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: your wet, yet somehow flaky hand
jokes.setup: Jack complaining about helping Liz
jokes.punchline: Specific and contradictory description of Liz's hand
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific insult - contradictory adjectives that somehow make perfect sense

jokes.text: I became the American auto industry failing to recognize that you can't fix a lemon!
jokes.setup: Jack explaining how Liz ruined his career
jokes.punchline: Extended metaphor using Liz's last name as car term
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extended metaphor that works on multiple levels - auto industry/lemon car/Liz Lemon

jokes.text: She threw a birthday party for her TV
jokes.setup: Pete explaining why Liz shouldn't be fired
jokes.punchline: Specific example of Liz's pathetic lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Perfect specific detail that captures Liz's loneliness in concrete terms

jokes.text: Is this just to talk, or will you be activating my electric underwear?
jokes.setup: Paul meeting Jenna during his break
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to bizarre sexual practice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Paul weirdness. The casual delivery makes the bizarre content funnier.

jokes.text: Right now it's between 'Frisbee-face,' and 'Glok-Gender-Irrelevant'
jokes.setup: Jenna talking about baby names for career purposes
jokes.punchline: Absurd celebrity baby name options
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of celebrity baby naming trends with Jenna's specific brand of crazy

jokes.text: Right now it's between 'Frisbee-face,' and 'Glok-Gender-Irrelevant.'
jokes.setup: Jenna planning crazy baby name for career attention
jokes.punchline: Two equally ridiculous name options
jokes.explanation: Perfect celebrity baby name parody. Both names are absurd in different ways.

jokes.text: I want to hog-tie you and hide you in the luggage compartment of a Greyhound bus with just a bowl of dirty water.
jokes.setup: Paul expressing love for Jenna
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific kidnapping fantasy as romantic expression
jokes.explanation: Outstanding Paul L'Astname moment. The specificity and darkness delivered as romance is perfect.

jokes.text: I want to hog-tie you and hide you in the luggage compartment of a Greyhound bus with just a bowl of dirty water
jokes.setup: Paul declaring his love for Jenna
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and disturbing romantic fantasy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Paul weirdness - romantic sentiment expressed through disturbing specificity

jokes.text: Denis, it's Dummy. I miss you, come see me
jokes.setup: Denis claiming Liz called him
jokes.punchline: Embarrassing voicemail with pet name
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Liz's gas-induced vulnerability

jokes.text: Waaaz aaap! You've reached Denis's voice mail. How you doing? Get out of here, Joey! I'm recording my voice mail message.
jokes.setup: Denis's voicemail message being played
jokes.punchline: Unprofessional message with interruption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Denis character moment - unprofessional but endearing in its authenticity

jokes.text: And us Duffys, we didn't give up when we got kicked out of Ireland. We didn't give up when America sent us back. And we didn't give up when Ireland then just set us adrift on a log, all right?
jokes.setup: Denis explaining his family's persistence
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd family history of rejection
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating absurdity - each rejection gets more ridiculous and specific

jokes.text: us Duffys, we didn't give up when we got kicked out of Ireland. We didn't give up when America sent us back. And we didn't give up when Ireland then just set us adrift on a log, all right?
jokes.setup: Denis claiming his family doesn't give up
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd family history of rejection
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating family history. The log detail is perfectly absurd and the structure builds well.

tmdb_overview: With their show targeted for cancellation, the "TGS" crew desperately scramble to mount a top-notch production for their 100th episode. Meanwhile, Jack reconsiders a vital decision in his life, and Jenna mulls over motherhood.
insight: Season 5's densest episode delivers 67 jokes in absurdist escalation without breaking pace.
